purpose: Set-level distinct-CLUSTER diversity (NoveltyBench axis): partitions a POOL of N responses to one prompt into near-duplicate equivalence clusters and reports the PARTITION — the distinct-cluster count (n_clusters), the cluster-size distribution (the 7-key n/mean/sd/min/p10/p50/p90 block), the explicit sorted cluster_sizes, a distinct_ratio (n_clusters / n_texts, oriented gt = MORE distinct), a utility-weighted distinctness at an operator-supplied discount, and one POSITIONAL representative per cluster (the earliest member by input order, with size + member_ids + an excerpt). NEVER a single "diversity score" — the distribution + representatives ARE the read. M1 lens = lexical-near-dup (stdlib word-shingle Jaccard >= threshold + single-link transitive closure; model-free, no numpy, deterministic). Clean-room of the set-partition read from NoveltyBench (arXiv:2504.05228); the paper's learned deduper is the POC-gated M2 --lens model-dedup seam. NOT an AI/human determination and NOT a model-quality call: measurements only, NO absolute band (like homogeneity_audit / originality_audit), no verdict, never a selection signal; the claim-license refuses any provenance call, any "mode-collapsed model" determination, and any claim that a representative is the "best" output. Distinct from its surface-mate skeleton_overlap_audit: this uses a CONTENT near-dup relation (word-shingle Jaccard) and reports a FULL partition INCLUDING singletons (how many distinct things were said); skeleton uses a STRUCTURAL discourse-template LCS relation and drops singletons (which docs reuse a template).
use_when: You have a prompt-matched POOL of responses to ONE prompt and want its discrete distinct-cluster structure — how many genuinely distinct things were said + what each distinct thing looks like
You want a partition + per-cluster representatives that the continuous pairwise-cosine of homogeneity_audit cannot give (a count + representatives, not a spread)
The pool has at least min_set (default 10) texts of >= ~15 words each
do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human call or a "this model is mode-collapsed / lacks diversity" determination (low distinctness is NOT a model defect — a tight topical prompt / shared genre / single source collapses clusters with no model involvement)
The pool answers MIXED prompts (inflates apparent distinctness) or a single tight topical prompt (deflates it), or you have fewer than the set floor
You want the paper's learned-deduper figures (M1 is a stdlib lexical near-dup lens — NOT paper-comparable; the model/embedding deduper is a gated M2 seam)

inputs.pool: a --manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path) OR a --dir of texts (.txt/.md, recursive)

compute.cost_note: M1 pure-stdlib lexical-near-dup lens (word-shingle Jaccard + single-link union-find); no models, no numpy, no API, deterministic. Strictly lighter than homogeneity_audit (which needs numpy only for effective_modes). The learned/embedding deduper (--lens model-dedup) is a POC-gated M2 seam (lazy-import + fail-loud missing_dependency), not in this build.

examples.description: Distinct-cluster diversity over a manifest of prompt-matched responses
Distinct-cluster diversity over a directory of pooled texts, stricter near-dup threshold

references: NoveltyBench — Evaluating Language Models for Humanlike Diversity (arXiv:2504.05228): https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.05228
